evals.prompt: I'm upgrading my MatrixScan Capacitor app from Scandit SDK v6 to v7. The app uses the old BarcodeTracking API. Here's my scanner file: MatrixScanApp_v6.js. Apply the necessary changes.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads migration.md and applies the v6→v7 BarcodeTracking→BarcodeBatch rename across all classes and the import: BarcodeTracking→BarcodeBatch, BarcodeTrackingSettings→BarcodeBatchSettings, BarcodeTrackingBasicOverlay→BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay, BarcodeTrackingBasicOverlayStyle→BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle. Bumps the package versions and reminds to run npx cap sync. Summarizes the changes made.

assertions.text: In the migrated code blocks, BarcodeBatch is used as the capture mode class
In the migrated code blocks, BarcodeBatchSettings is used (not BarcodeTrackingSettings)
In the migrated code blocks, BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay is used (not BarcodeTrackingBasicOverlay)
In the migrated code blocks, BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle is used for the overlay style
In the migrated code blocks, no class name starting with BarcodeTracking remains
The import from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode' is updated to the BarcodeBatch* names
The migration mentions bumping the scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core and scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode package versions
The migration reminds the user to run npx cap sync after the version bump (semantic)
The migration notes the underlying API behavior is unchanged — only the class names differ (semantic)
A summary of the changes made is shown

evals.prompt: We're moving our MatrixScan Batch Capacitor app from Scandit SDK 7 to 8. Here's our code: MatrixScanApp_v7.js. Please apply any necessary changes.
evals.expected_output: The skill applies the v7→v8 changes: DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey → DataCaptureContext.initialize, and BarcodeBatch.forContext(context, settings) → new BarcodeBatch(settings) followed by context.setMode(barcodeBatch). Reminds to bump package versions and run npx cap sync. Summarizes the changes.

assertions.text: In the migrated code blocks, DataCaptureContext.initialize( is used to create the context
In the migrated code blocks, DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is not used
In the migrated code blocks, new BarcodeBatch(settings) constructs the mode
In the migrated code blocks, BarcodeBatch.forContext( is not used
context.setMode( is called with the barcodeBatch instance after constructing it
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is still placed after await ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() (semantic)
The migration reminds the user to run npx cap sync after the version bump (semantic)
A summary of the changes made is shown

evals.prompt: We need to upgrade our MatrixScan integration from Scandit SDK 6 all the way to v8. Here's our current code: MatrixScanApp_v6.js
evals.expected_output: The skill applies both migrations in order (6→7 then 7→8): the BarcodeTracking→BarcodeBatch rename, then DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey→initialize and BarcodeBatch.forContext→new BarcodeBatch + context.setMode. Summarizes both migration steps.

assertions.text: In the migrated code blocks, BarcodeBatch is used as the capture mode class (6→7 rename applied)
In the migrated code blocks, no class name starting with BarcodeTracking remains
In the migrated code blocks, new BarcodeBatch(settings) constructs the mode (7→8 factory change applied)
In the migrated code blocks, BarcodeBatch.forContext( is not used
In the migrated code blocks, DataCaptureContext.initialize( is used (7→8 rename applied)
In the migrated code blocks, DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is not used
context.setMode( is called with the barcodeBatch instance
The summary covers both migration steps (references both 6→7 and 7→8)
